element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns to identify monsters and match them with bombs in simple configurations
Player learns bombs must match monster color; introduced alongside monsters in initial rounds
Some monsters require two matches in quick succession or diagonal matches; complexity increases after basic mechanics established
Descending Thwomp disguised as ceiling reduces playfield space; introduces time pressure and spatial constraint tension
Player learns Toad can climb stacks and manipulate height; essential for accessing trapped pieces and managing field
Cascade mechanics send enemy stacks to opponent; introduces asymmetric pressure and chain reaction strategy

typical_relief_triggers: Successfully matching three or more objects (bomb + matching monsters)
Clearing the playfield of all monsters
typical_tension_triggers: Introduction of new monster types with special requirements
Thwomp descending and reducing available space
Monsters accumulating faster than player can clear
Game Over condition: field fills to brim and traps Toad
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive escalation: each round increases initial monster count; space compression via Thwomp creates episodic tension peaks; multiplayer mode introduces simultaneous pressure from opponent cascades

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of basic monsters and bomb-matching; simple color identification; minimal spatial constraint; learning curve for Toad manipulation mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Monster type variation introduces special destruction requirements; Thwomp ceiling begins appearing; stack height management becomes strategic; increased monster density per round
difficulty_curve.late_game: Combination of multiple special monster types; rapid Thwomp descent; high monster spawn rate; requires expert timing, spatial prediction, and stack optimization
notable_spikes: Introduction of Thwomp mechanic (space compression creates sudden pressure shift)
First appearance of special monster types requiring compound matches
Late-round playfield density: field fills rapidly relative to match opportunities

notable_level_patterns.description: Each successful level clears playfield but advances to next round with increased initial monster count; creates progressive difficulty curve without discrete level design, instead using parametric escalation
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Round 1→Round 2 transition shows monster count increase

notable_level_patterns.description: Mid-game rounds introduce Thwomp ceiling that descends periodically, reducing vertical playfield space and forcing player to work faster; creates episodic tension within rounds
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mid-game rounds (specific round numbers not documented)

notable_level_patterns.description: Special monster types requiring specific match configurations (diagonal, consecutive, two-quick-succession) introduced gradually; forces player adaptation to new puzzle rules within existing round structure
VS Mode allows players to send stacked enemy pieces to opponent via chain matches; creates bidirectional threat and introduces external pressure source beyond field mechanics
